metadata.title: Conversion Successful Is Not a Promise

beats.narration_text: Habari, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The toolchain printed success at every step — and the deployed model's predictions were garbage.

props.command: My model converted successfully through every stage but on-device accuracy dropped from 91% to 72%. What happened?
output: Green gates don't mean correct output. The only truth test is running the same inputs at every stage and bisecting whichever accuracy gap appears.

beats.narration_text: From the book: the six-step deployment pipeline with dominant failure modes annotated at each stage. Training passes. Conversion passes. The silent failure often lives in optimize or compile.

Here's the pipeline. Six gates: source model, ONNX export, TFLite float conversion, int8 calibration, device compiler, deployed model. Every gate printed success. No error codes. No warnings. Green all the way through. The toolchain did exactly what it was designed to do — it reported on whether the conversion completed, not on whether the result was correct.

The accuracy trace. Source model: 91.5%. ONNX export: 91.0% — tiny rounding. TFLite float: still 91.0%. Int8 calibration: 89.8% — acceptable quantization loss. Device: 72%. That's a 17.8-point drop in one step, under a green success banner. An unsupported op fusion silently produced wrong outputs. The toolchain compiled it. The compiler didn't validate numerical equivalence.

Bisection: run the same test inputs through every stage. Compare outputs. The gap between int8 and device is where accuracy falls off the cliff. The bracket closes there. Now you know which conversion to examine. Unsupported op fusion — a GELU approximated incorrectly at int8. The fix is one line: disable the fusion, or replace with a supported op. You cannot find this without a numerical trace at every stage.

Your turn. Ask Claude: I converted my model through open bracket toolchain close bracket and my on-device accuracy dropped from open bracket source acc close bracket percent to open bracket device acc close bracket percent. Walk me through: one — how to set up a bisection test at every pipeline stage, PyTorch to ONNX to TFLite float to TFLite int8 to device. Two — what metadata to compare at each stage: output logits, top-1 accuracy. Three — common causes of sudden accuracy drops at the TFLite int8 step. Four — how to identify unsupported op fusions.

props.command: I converted my model through [toolchain] and my on-device accuracy dropped from [source_acc]% to [device_acc]%. Walk me through: (1) how to set up a bisection test at every pipeline stage (PyTorch → ONNX → TFLite float → TFLite int8 → device), (2) what metadata to compare at each stage (output logits, top-1 accuracy), (3) common causes of sudden accuracy drops at the TFLite int8 step, (4) how to identify unsupported op fusions.
output: Source: 91.5% → ONNX: 91.0% → TFLite float: 91.0% → int8: 89.8% → device: 72%. Catastrophic drop at device — unsupported op fusion.

props.title: Conversion Successful Is Not a Promise
